metadata.title: Product Experiences
metadata.description: Outcome-based product experiences for the merged Internal domain, covering revenue operations, employee productivity, finance control, governance, workplace coordination, and enterprise resilience.

definitions.productBrick: A reusable implementation-facing building block used to realize one or more product experiences.
definitions.productCapability: A high-level, outcome-based experience that delivers customer or business value.

metadata.flowModelNote: Each experience now carries only experience-specific flows. Each flow step lists the bricks and external systems used inside that experience.

capabilities.name: See One Revenue Picture
capabilities.description: Help revenue operations users work from a single, trusted view of accounts, opportunities, and revenue data.

flows.title: See One Revenue Picture
flows.description: Help revenue operations users work from a single, trusted view of accounts, opportunities, and revenue data.

steps.title: Open revenue workspace
keyFacts: Open revenue workspace is a meaningful step in the revenue operations user experience flow.
Users need clear guidance and feedback here to keep moving forward.
painPoints: Users drop out when open revenue workspace feels fragmented across channels.
Confidence decreases when the product does not explain what happens next.

capabilities.name: Move Work Through Approvals

flows.title: Move Work Through Approvals
flows.description: Help internal users move work forward through required approvals and exception paths without losing context.

steps.title: Submit workflow step
keyFacts: This step converts intent into a real transaction or commitment.
Trust, clarity, and feedback matter more here than feature breadth.
painPoints: Users hesitate when they do not trust the outcome, price, or handoff.
Small friction at this point creates disproportionate conversion loss.

capabilities.name: Self-Serve Common Needs
capabilities.description: Help employees submit requests, track progress, and finish common internal service work without manual chasing.
outcomes: Lower employee effort to get help

flows.title: Self-Serve Common Needs
flows.description: Guide an employee from request submission to transparent resolution.

steps.title: Start the right request
keyFacts: Users need guidance before they submit incomplete work.
painPoints: Bad submissions create avoidable back-and-forth.

steps.title: Apply approval logic
keyFacts: Approvals should happen automatically for common cases.
painPoints: Serial review chains create delay without increasing quality.

steps.title: Run the service case
keyFacts: Ownership and SLA need to stay visible end to end.
painPoints: Users lose confidence when queue status disappears.

steps.title: Close the loop
keyFacts: Resolution should leave behind reusable knowledge and measurable feedback.
painPoints: The same issue repeats if nothing is captured for the next cycle.

capabilities.name: Move Work Through Controls
capabilities.description: Help service operators move high-volume work through policy and approvals with fewer exceptions.
outcomes: Lower exception rate and backlog volatility

flows.title: Move Work Through Controls
flows.description: Guide an operator from normalized intake to controlled execution and measurement.

steps.title: Normalize intake
keyFacts: Quality at intake determines downstream throughput.
painPoints: Broken submissions create expensive manual correction.

steps.title: Route and approve
keyFacts: Approvals should enforce policy without serial overhead.
painPoints: Operators lose capacity when they become manual routers.

steps.title: Execute spend or vendor work
keyFacts: Finance and procurement actions need clean handoff into downstream systems.
painPoints: Rekeying and missing fields create preventable exceptions.

steps.title: Watch exceptions and SLA risk
keyFacts: Leaders need early visibility of queue or policy drift.
painPoints: Escalations come too late when metrics are stale or fragmented.

capabilities.name: Control Spend To Close
capabilities.description: Help finance operators move from spend requests and invoices to reconciled close status with fewer manual handoffs.

flows.title: Control Spend To Close
flows.description: Guide finance operations from committed spend and invoice intake to reconciled close and cost visibility.

steps.title: Capture committed spend
keyFacts: Finance needs the commitment picture before cash leaves the business.
painPoints: Spend control weakens when requests, vendors, and approvals fragment before invoice receipt.

steps.title: Match invoice and expense flows
keyFacts: Invoice and reimbursement exceptions should surface in one operating thread.
painPoints: AP and employee reimbursement drift apart when they use unrelated control surfaces.

steps.title: Close and allocate spend
keyFacts: Close quality depends on reconciliations, allocations, and task visibility moving together.
painPoints: Finance loses time when close status, cost allocation, and operating explanations live in separate tools.

capabilities.name: See Cash And Plan With Confidence
capabilities.description: Help finance leaders and business partners see cash position, cost outlook, and scenario changes in one trusted planning surface.
outcomes: Higher confidence in cash and forecast decisions
Faster scenario planning across workforce and spend changes

flows.title: See Cash And Plan With Confidence
flows.description: Guide a finance leader from cash visibility to scenario planning and decision follow-through.

steps.title: Open cash and commitment picture
keyFacts: Liquidity and committed spend need one operating view.
painPoints: Treasury decisions slow down when cash, invoices, and committed spend reconcile too late.

steps.title: Model budget and scenario changes
keyFacts: Forecast quality improves when spend, workforce, and allocation changes share one scenario model.
painPoints: Finance and operating leaders revert to spreadsheets when planning surfaces do not connect to live operating signals.

steps.title: Commit decisions and track impact
keyFacts: Finance decisions need clear status in close, metrics, and downstream approvals.
painPoints: Leaders lose trust when scenarios do not flow back into actuals and operational controls.

capabilities.name: Diagnose and Resolve with Context

capabilities.name: Prove Compliance with Less Effort
capabilities.description: Help control owners gather evidence, test controls, and remediate findings without heavy manual overhead.

flows.title: Prove Compliance with Less Effort
flows.description: Help control owners gather evidence, test controls, and remediate findings without heavy manual overhead.

steps.title: Collect evidence
keyFacts: Collect evidence is a meaningful step in the governance user experience flow.
Users need clear guidance and feedback here to keep moving forward.
painPoints: Users drop out when collect evidence feels fragmented across channels.
Confidence decreases when the product does not explain what happens next.

dependencies.name: Control evidence ledger

steps.title: Run control test

painPoints: Users drop out when run control test feels fragmented across channels.

dependencies.name: Audit and Compliance Systems
dependencies.description: Consume evidence and control outputs.
dependencies.how: Relies on Audit and Compliance Systems for consume evidence and control outputs in run control test.

steps.title: Review finding
keyFacts: This step shapes first relevance and perceived product quality.
Users decide quickly whether the marketplace understands their intent.
painPoints: Low relevance or weak filtering creates early abandonment.
Users lose trust when the result set feels noisy or poorly explained.

dependencies.name: Automated controls testing
dependencies.description: Runs repeatable tests and flags failures or anomalies for review.
dependencies.how: Uses Automated controls testing for runs repeatable tests and flags failures or anomalies for review in review finding.

dependencies.name: Audit and Compliance Systems
dependencies.description: Consume evidence and control outputs.
dependencies.how: Relies on Audit and Compliance Systems for consume evidence and control outputs in review finding.

steps.title: Launch remediation action
keyFacts: This step determines how quickly users reach initial value.
It often combines setup effort, policy choices, and correctness checks.
painPoints: Users stall when setup asks for too much context too early.
Errors here create rework and reduce confidence in the product model.

capabilities.name: Prove Control Readiness
capabilities.description: Help legal, compliance, and audit operators move from fragmented evidence gathering to continuously assembled control readiness.
outcomes: Faster audit and policy review cycles

flows.title: Prove Control Readiness
flows.description: Guide a compliance or audit operator from policy context to assembled evidence and clear remediation.

steps.title: Open policy and control context
keyFacts: Operators need one control catalog and one policy source of truth.
painPoints: Control drift grows when policy, ownership, and testing status are disconnected.

steps.title: Assemble evidence continuously
keyFacts: Evidence is more valuable when it is collected during the workflow rather than before the audit.
painPoints: Manual evidence collection delays audit and weakens confidence in completeness.

steps.title: Resolve privacy and retention gaps
keyFacts: Privacy and records issues need to connect back to the same workflow and owners.
painPoints: Separate privacy and records work creates parallel remediation paths.

capabilities.name: Protect and Recover Enterprise Work
capabilities.description: Help IT and security operators resolve incidents, track exposure, and keep enterprise work resilient.
outcomes: Clearer view of operational and security risk

flows.title: Protect and Recover Enterprise Work
flows.description: Guide security and IT operators from detection and triage to recovery and follow-up.

steps.title: Open incident and asset context
keyFacts: Triage is faster when service, asset, and owner context are already connected.
painPoints: Response slows down when teams must reconstruct relationships between incidents, devices, and owners.

steps.title: Prioritize risk and containment
keyFacts: Exposure and identity risk need to be visible in the same operational thread.
painPoints: Teams lose time when vulnerability and identity exceptions sit outside incident command.

steps.title: Recover and prove readiness
keyFacts: Resilience depends on tested recovery plans, not just incident closure.
painPoints: Post-incident learning is weak when recovery readiness and operating evidence are not captured.

capabilities.name: See Enterprise Health
capabilities.description: Help enterprise leaders see one reliable operating picture across employee work, service flow, and automation value.

flows.title: See Enterprise Health
flows.description: Guide a leader from KPI view to decision narrative and action.

steps.title: Open the metric layer
keyFacts: Leaders need one semantics layer, not competing reports.
painPoints: Metric debates consume the time meant for decisions.

steps.title: Connect narrative context
keyFacts: Decision quality improves when KPI changes carry explanation and action history.
painPoints: Leadership reviews become slideware when operating context is missing.

steps.title: Inspect work and capacity
keyFacts: Leaders need to see whether people and service operations are aligned.
painPoints: Separate workforce and service views hide system-level constraints.

steps.title: Decide and follow through
keyFacts: Decisions need explicit ownership and measurable next steps.
painPoints: Reviews fail when decisions do not flow back into operations.

capabilities.name: Automate Work Safely
capabilities.description: Help platform admins design, deploy, and govern reusable enterprise automations safely.
outcomes: Higher automation reuse with lower risk

flows.title: Automate Work Safely
flows.description: Guide an administrator from workflow design to policy-safe production monitoring.

steps.title: Model the workflow
keyFacts: Reusable templates lower rollout cost and improve consistency.
painPoints: Custom per-flow design raises long-term maintenance cost.

steps.title: Connect the systems
keyFacts: Identity and event context are required for safe cross-system automation.
painPoints: Automation breaks when authoritative data changes are missed.

steps.title: Apply guardrails
keyFacts: AI and automation need review thresholds, audit trails, and exception paths.
painPoints: Trust collapses quickly when automation is fast but not explainable.

steps.title: Monitor production value and reliability
keyFacts: Admins need both operational reliability and business impact to prioritize improvements.
painPoints: Automation teams over-focus on deployment without proving enterprise value.

capabilities.name: Start Work Fast
capabilities.description: Help employees open one workspace, see the right tasks, and move work forward quickly.
outcomes: Less time spent finding the next task
Higher completion inside the guided workflow

flows.title: Start Work Fast
flows.description: Guide an employee from identity-aware landing to completed work and follow-up.

steps.title: Resolve employee context
keyFacts: The experience starts with the right identity and org context.
painPoints: Users lose trust if the workspace is personalized incorrectly.

steps.title: Open the work hub
keyFacts: The hub should prioritize the next action, not just list everything.
painPoints: Too much noise creates the same fragmentation as legacy tools.

steps.title: Get embedded guidance
keyFacts: Knowledge and prior decisions need to appear in context.
painPoints: Search-only help is too slow for interruption-heavy work.

steps.title: Complete in the right channel
keyFacts: Some work finishes in web, some in mobile or messaging.
painPoints: Users abandon flows when they have to return to a desktop unnecessarily.

capabilities.name: Run Team With Clarity
capabilities.description: Help managers act on team workload, approvals, and growth signals from one operating surface.
outcomes: Faster action on team risks and goals

flows.title: Run Team With Clarity
flows.description: Guide a manager from team state awareness to decision and follow-up.

steps.title: Open team operating view
keyFacts: Managers need workload and people state in one place.
painPoints: Disconnected planning and people tools hide the real operating picture.

steps.title: Complete manager actions
keyFacts: Approvals and follow-ups must be lightweight.
painPoints: Managers lose time if every action requires tool switching.

steps.title: Coach and grow the team
keyFacts: Goals, learning, and feedback should stay tied to live work context.
painPoints: Growth loops decay when they live outside the work system.

steps.title: Measure and escalate
keyFacts: Escalations should carry both KPI and qualitative context.
painPoints: Managers struggle when they have metrics but not the story behind them.

capabilities.name: Coordinate Place and Communication
capabilities.description: Help employees and workplace operators coordinate rooms, access, events, and communication in one enterprise workflow layer.
outcomes: Higher visibility of internal communication reach and response

flows.title: Coordinate Place and Communication
flows.description: Guide employees and workplace operators from booking and access through communication and follow-up.

steps.title: Reserve the right place
keyFacts: Place coordination starts with a booking flow that already knows employee context and site rules.
painPoints: Employees lose time when booking, access, and event planning live in separate tools.

steps.title: Coordinate the event or gathering
keyFacts: Events need attendance, logistics, and communication to move as one workflow.
painPoints: Internal events turn into spreadsheet work when orchestration and communications are detached.

steps.title: Govern channels and gather pulse
keyFacts: Communication quality depends on channel governance and visible feedback loops.
painPoints: Broadcasts become noise when channels are unmanaged and no sentiment loop exists.

capabilities.name: Join and Onboard Smoothly
capabilities.description: Help candidates, new hires, and managers move from recruiting to onboarding with clear handoffs, complete profile data, and minimal setup friction.

flows.title: Join and Onboard Smoothly
flows.description: Help candidates, new hires, and managers move from recruiting to onboarding with clear handoffs, complete profile data, and minimal setup friction.
